amazonmq.broker.user.CfnAbout: The list of broker users (persons or applications) who can access queues and topics. For Amazon MQ for RabbitMQ brokers, one and only one administrative user is accepted and created when a broker is first provisioned. All subsequent broker users are created via the RabbitMQ web console or by using the RabbitMQ management API.

amazonmq.broker.user.ConsoleAccess.Description: Enables access to the ActiveMQ web console for the ActiveMQ user. Does not apply to RabbitMQ brokers.

amazonmq.broker.user.Groups.Description: The list of groups (20 maximum) to which the ActiveMQ user belongs. This value can contain only alphanumeric characters, dashes, periods, underscores, and tildes (- . _ ~). This value must be 2-100 characters long. Does not apply to RabbitMQ brokers.

amazonmq.broker.user.Password.Description: The password of the user. This value must be at least 12 characters long, must contain at least 4 unique characters, and must not contain commas, colons, or equal signs (,:=).

amazonmq.broker.user.Username.Description: The username of the broker user. For Amazon MQ for ActiveMQ brokers, this value can contain only alphanumeric characters, dashes, periods, underscores, and tildes (- . _ ~). For Amazon MQ for RabbitMQ brokers, this value can contain only alphanumeric characters, dashes, periods, underscores (- . _). This value must not contain a tilde (~) character. Amazon MQ prohibts using guest as a valid usename. This value must be 2-100 characters long.   Do not add personally identifiable information (PII) or other confidential or sensitive information in broker usernames. Broker usernames are accessible to other AWS services, including CloudWatch Logs. Broker usernames are not intended to be used for private or sensitive data.
